task.title: Reject stored stream-version gaps before append
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/410 by rejecting a stored SQLite event-stream version gap before caller payload encoding while preserving existing invalid-version and terminal range-error precedence.
acceptance_criteria: Append detects an internal stored stream-version gap before expected-version matching and payload encoding.
The failure is EventLogError::VersionGap with the missing immediate predecessor and the first observed version that lacks it.
The rejected append writes no event.
The immediate write transaction repeats continuity validation so concurrent state cannot bypass it.
Existing invalid-version, exhausted-stream, append, and replay behavior remains intact, documentation is updated, and pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused regression with stored stream versions one and three plus a serializer that must not run.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed to compile because EventLogError had no VersionGap variant, demonstrating that append had no explicit continuity failure contract.

attempts.summary: Added an append-side VersionGap error and queried the first stored version whose immediate predecessor is missing.
attempts.patch: The focused regression returned VersionGap { expected: 2, found: 3 } before serialization and confirmed the stored row count remained two; the same current-position helper runs during preflight and inside the immediate transaction.

attempts.summary: Ran the complete event-log suite and corrected error precedence for a terminal SQLite stream.
attempts.diagnostic: The existing exhausted-stream regression received VersionGap for its singleton i64::MAX fixture instead of the documented VersionOutOfRange failure, because continuity validation ran before terminal range handling.
attempts.patch: Recorded the full-suite diagnostic before changing production precedence.

attempts.summary: Refactored current-position validation to preserve terminal range-error precedence while retaining invalid-version and ordinary gap detection.
attempts.diagnostic: The full 25-test event-log suite passed; SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN showed the stream range searches and correlated predecessor probes using the events primary key.
attempts.patch: After validating the minimum stored version, return the terminal maximum to the existing checked next-version path before reporting earlier continuity gaps; document the rationale beside the branch.

attempts.summary: Completed independent review, required pull-request checks, automated review, and squash merge.
attempts.diagnostic: Independent read-only review found no blocking issue and suggested an additional terminal-precedence test, but the existing singleton-i64::MAX fixture necessarily has missing predecessors and had already failed before the correction, so it directly pins that branch. CodeRabbit passed with one trivial suggestion for a separate start-above-one append fixture. The merged SQL predicate handles that state by finding the first stored row without its immediate predecessor, but the append path does not have a dedicated regression for that distinct fixture. Rust quality gate run 29337632910 and CodeRabbit passed; pull request 411 then auto-merged as da355d5 before any review revision.
attempts.patch: No post-review revision was included before automatic merge; the unadded start-above-one append regression remains an explicit coverage limitation.

outcome.summary: Append now reports the first stored stream-version gap before caller serialization and rechecks continuity atomically, while preserving explicit invalid stored-version and terminal SQLite range failures.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_a_stored_version_gap_before_encoding -- --exact
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log
SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN for append continuity lookup: stream range searches and predecessor probes use the events primary key
Independent pre-commit read-only diff review for issue 410: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29337632910 for pull request 411
Required CodeRabbit status and completed review for pull request 411
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0022-append-version-gap.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 22 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: A maximum stored version is insufficient to prove that an append-only stream is contiguous; lower invalid rows and missing predecessors require explicit validation.
A serializer that must not run makes validation order observable and protects callers from unnecessary or unsafe work on rejected appends.
Unlocked preflight improves deterministic early failure, but the same invariant must be checked again inside the authoritative immediate transaction.
Full-suite tests preserve error precedence across overlapping corruption and range conditions that a focused GREEN test may not expose.

Review suggestions should be checked against exact fixture semantics: the singleton maximum-version fixture already exercises terminal range precedence over a missing predecessor, while a non-terminal start-above-one stream remains a distinct unpinned append case.
Query-plan evidence distinguishes an indexed integrity check from an accidental unbounded table scan, even when validating a stream range remains linear in that stream's length.
